questions.text:
A flower shop has a room that has an air-conditioning system. It is used to keep the room at a constant temperature for storing flowers. The owner uses a keypad to set the temperature for the room.

Tick (✓) one box to show whether a keypad is an example of an input, output, process or storage device.

A input
B output
C process
D storage

marking_points.text: A

questions.text:
A flower shop has a room that has an air-conditioning system. The owner of the shop enters a denary number to set the temperature for the room. The denary number is converted to a binary number to be processed. The binary number is stored in a register in the air conditioning system.

Give the binary number that would be stored when the denary number 24 is entered.

A flower shop has a room that has an air-conditioning system. The owner of the shop enters a denary number to set the temperature for the room. The denary number is converted to a binary number to be processed. The binary number is stored in a register in the air conditioning system.

Give the binary number that would be stored when the denary number 101 is entered.

A flower shop has a room that has an air-conditioning system. The owner of the shop enters a denary number to set the temperature for the room. The denary number is converted to a binary number to be processed. The binary number is stored in a register in the air conditioning system.

The binary number 00010011 is stored in the register. Give the denary number that has been entered for this binary number to be stored.

A flower shop has a room that has an air-conditioning system. The owner of the shop enters a denary number to set the temperature for the room. The denary number is converted to a binary number to be processed. The binary number is stored in a register in the air conditioning system.

The binary number 00100010 is stored in the register. Give the denary number that has been entered for this binary number to be stored.

If the air-conditioning system detects an error, it displays an error code. The error code is displayed as a hexadecimal number. The binary number for each error code is stored in a register.

Give the binary number that is stored in the register to display the error code 51.

If the air-conditioning system detects an error, it displays an error code. The error code is displayed as a hexadecimal number. The binary number for each error code is stored in a register.

Give the binary number that is stored in the register to display the error code E3.

If the air-conditioning system detects an error, it displays an error code. The error code is displayed as a hexadecimal number. The binary number for each error code is stored in a register.

Give the hexadecimal number that is displayed when the binary number 10000010 is stored in the register.

If the owner of the shop wants to make the room very cold, she may need to enter a negative denary number. The negative denary number could be represented using two's complement.

Give the two's complement 8-bit binary integer that would be stored for the temperature –5. Show all your working.

Working space

Two's complement 8-bit binary integer [blank]

answers.scoring_rule: 1 mark for correct method of working, 1 mark for correct answer

marking_points.text: One mark for correct method of working, for example flip and add 1
One mark for correct answer 11111011

questions.text:
The air-conditioning system uses a temperature sensor and a microprocessor to keep the temperature of the room at a constant level.

Tick (✓) one box to show whether the sensor is an example of an input, output, process or storage device.

A input
B output
C process
D storage

marking_points.text: A

questions.text:
The air-conditioning system uses a temperature sensor and a microprocessor to keep the temperature of the room at a constant level. The temperature sensor continuously sends analogue data to the microprocessor. This data is then converted to digital.

Explain how the microprocessor keeps the temperature of the room at a constant level.

marking_points.text: Microprocessor compares the value from the sensor to a stored value
If the values do not match, the microprocessor sends a signal to increase or decrease the temperature
If the values match, no action is taken

questions.text:
The air-conditioning system is an example of an embedded system.

Explain why the air-conditioning system is an embedded system.

marking_points.text: It has a single/limited function
It is difficult to change its function // cannot be reprogrammed
It has dedicated hardware
It has a microprocessor

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU) that carries out the fetch-decode-execute (FDE) cycle.

Give the name of the first register that is used in the fetch part of the cycle.

marking_points.text: Program counter // PC

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU) that carries out the fetch-decode-execute (FDE) cycle. The CPU has a control unit (CU).

Describe the role of the CU in the CPU.

marking_points.text: It sends control signals to other components
… using the control bus
… to manage the flow of data through the CPU
It decodes an instruction
… using an instruction set

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU) that carries out the fetch-decode-execute (FDE) cycle. The CPU has a control unit (CU).

Give the name of the register that is part of the CU.

marking_points.text: Current instruction register // CIR

questions.text:
The computer has primary storage. One example of primary storage is cache.

Tick (✓) one box to show the correct statement that describes the purpose of cache.

A It stores important instructions.
B It stores the next instruction to be processed.
C It stores frequently used instructions.
D It stores instructions so they are not lost when the power is turned off.

rows: B It stores the next instruction to be processed.
C It stores frequently used instructions.
D It stores instructions so they are not lost when the power is turned off.

marking_points.text: C

questions.text:
The computer has primary storage. One example of primary storage is cache.

Give one other example of primary storage. Identify the type of data your example would store.

Primary storage
Data

answers.scoring_rule:
One mark for example of storage
One mark for example of what it will store

marking_points.text: Primary storage: RAM // ROM // BIOS // Virtual memory

Data: Data/programs/software/instructions that are currently in use (for RAM) // Startup instructions / Bootstrap / Firmware (for ROM/BIOS) // Pages from RAM that are not immediately needed / Data/programs/software/instructions that are currently in use (for Virtual memory)

questions.text:
The computer also has secondary storage.

Write the name of the type of secondary storage for each description in the table. Each type of secondary storage may be used more than once.

Type of secondary storage | Description
(blank) | It burns pits and lands onto a disk.
(blank) | It uses control gates and flow gates.
(blank) | It uses NAND or NOR technology.
(blank) | The surface of each platter is divided into tracks and sectors.
(blank) | It has a read/write arm that moves a laser across a disc.
(blank) | It controls the flow of electrons using transistors.

rows.definition: It burns pits and lands onto a disk.
It uses control gates and flow gates.
It uses NAND or NOR technology.
The surface of each platter is divided into tracks and sectors.
It has a read/write arm that moves a laser across a disc.
It controls the flow of electrons using transistors.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct storage type

marking_points.text: It burns pits and lands onto a disk. — Optical
It uses control gates and flow gates. — Solid-state
It uses NAND or NOR technology. — Solid-state
The surface of each platter is divided into tracks and sectors. — Magnetic
It has a read/write arm that moves a laser across a disc. — Optical
It controls the flow of electrons using transistors. — Solid-state

questions.text:
Data is split into packets before it is transmitted across a network.

The diagram shows a packet with three sections. Complete the diagram by writing the name of each section of the packet. Write an example in each section of what would be stored in that part of the packet.

Packet

First
The first section is called the [blank] Example [blank]

Second
The second section is called the [blank] Example [blank]

Third
The third section is called the [blank] Example [blank]
visuals: Packet diagram with three sections labelled First, Second, Third
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

visuals: Packet diagram with three sections labelled First, Second, Third
answers.scoring_rule:
One mark for each correct section
One mark for each correct example of what it stores

marking_points.text: First section name: Header
First section example: Originators/senders (IP) address // Destination/receivers (IP) address // Packet number
Second section name: Payload
Second section example: The data itself
Third section name: Trailer
Third section example: End of packet notification // Error checking data

questions.text:
Packets can be sent across a network using different methods of data transmission.

Explain how data is transmitted in the following method of data transmission:

Serial full-duplex
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Data is sent one bit at a time
… down a single wire
Data can be sent in both directions …
… at the same time

questions.text:
Packets can be sent across a network using different methods of data transmission.

Explain how data is transmitted in the following method of data transmission:

Parallel half-duplex
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Data is sent multiple bits at the same time
… down multiple wires
Data can be sent in both directions …
… not at the same time

questions.text:
A cyber security company specialises in security solutions that help prevent hackers being successful.

Identify two security solutions that can be provided to help prevent hackers being successful. Explain how each solution helps prevent hackers being successful.

Solution 1
Explanation

Solution 2
Explanation

answers.scoring_rule:
One mark for the security solution
Two marks for a matching description

marking_points.text: Strong/complex password
Requires a mixture of letters, number and symbols
Makes it very difficult for the hacker to guess
Limiting number of attempts for password
Cannot keep repeatedly guessing a password
Stops a brute-force attack being successful
Biometric device
Requires the hacker to have biometric data to access the data
It is very difficult to fake biometric data
Two-step verification // two-factor authentication
Requires the hacker to have additional data to access the data
The data is sent to the users registered device/account that the hacker would also need
Firewall
Examines incoming data/traffic
Can block any data that does not meet set criteria
Anti-spyware
Detects/removes any spyware present on the computer
Hacker cannot use spyware to get data to discover passwords

questions.text:
The company also has a web page that provides information to improve people's awareness of social engineering.

Describe what is meant by social engineering. Include an example of social engineering in your answer.

answers.scoring_rule: Two marks for: ... Any one from:

marking_points.text: Involves manipulating/deceiving people
… with the aim of obtaining confidential/personal/valuable data
For example: Phishing // by example // Baiting // by example // Shoulder surfing // by example

questions.text:
The company's website uses both session cookies and persistent cookies.

State one similarity between session cookies and persistent cookies.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: They are both small text files
They both store data about the user // by example
They are both stored/managed by the web browser
They are both downloaded on to a user's computer

questions.text:
The company's website uses both session cookies and persistent cookies.

Explain one difference between session cookies and persistent cookies.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

answers.scoring_rule: Two from (one mark for session, one mark for matching comparison to persistent):

marking_points.text: Session cookies are deleted when the web browser is closed
… whereas persistent cookies are deleted by the user/expire after a certain time
Session cookies are only stored in RAM
… whereas persistent cookies are stored in the secondary storage

questions.text:
Text, images and sound are represented as binary to be processed by a computer.

Complete the table by writing the missing terms and descriptions about representing text, images and sound as binary.

Term | Description
character set | (blank)
(blank) | This is the number of samples taken each second when recording sound.
pixel | (blank)
resolution | (blank)
(blank) | This is the number of bits used to represent a colour in an image.

rows.definition: This is the number of samples taken each second when recording sound.
This is the number of bits used to represent a colour in an image.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct term or description

marking_points.text: character set: This stores all the characters and their unique binary values
sample rate: This is the number of samples taken each second when recording sound.
pixel: This is the smallest unit/part of an image
resolution: This is the number of pixels that appear in an image/displayed on a screen // The number of pixels high by the number of pixels wide // the bit rate and sample rate of sound
colour depth // bit depth: This is the number of bits used to represent a colour in an image.

questions.text:
Text, images and sound are represented as binary to be processed by a computer.

The size of an image file is 1000 bytes. Give the size of the image file in bits.

marking_points.text: 8000 (bits)

questions.text:
Text, images and sound are represented as binary to be processed by a computer.

The size of a sound file is 4096 mebibytes (MiB). Give the size of the file in gibibytes (GiB).

marking_points.text: 4 (GiB)

questions.text:
A computer game is programmed to use artificial intelligence (AI) to create the movements and actions of the computer-controlled characters in the game.

Describe what is meant by AI.

marking_points.text: Simulation of intelligent behaviours by computers
Collection of data and rules for using that data
A computer that has the ability to learn
A computer that has the ability to adapt
A computer that has the ability to reason/make decisions

questions.text:
A computer game is programmed to use artificial intelligence (AI) to create the movements and actions of the computer-controlled characters in the game.

Explain how the AI can be used to improve the movements and actions of the computer-controlled characters.

marking_points.text: It could use machine learning
It can collect data about the players actions
Analyse/identify patterns in the player's movements
… so it can learn how the player moves
… to help predict the next movement of the player
It can adapt its own movements to match the players // It can adapt its own processes
It can store successful/unsuccessful moves
It will learn the most efficient/optimal movements against a player

questions.text:
The computer game is programmed using a high-level language.

Explain one difference between a high-level language and a low-level language.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

answers.scoring_rule: Two from (one mark for high-level, one mark for matching comparison to low-level)

marking_points.text: High-level language is written in English-like statements …
…low-level language is written using mnemonics/as machine code/in binary
High-level language is easier to read/write/debug …
… low-level language is more difficult to read/write/debug
High-level language is machine independent/portable …
…low-level language is not machine independent/portable
High-level language cannot directly manipulate the hardware …
… low-level language can directly manipulate the hardware
In high-level language, one line of code can perform multiple actions …
… in low-level language, one line of code performs a single action

questions.text:
The programmer uses translators to translate the high-level language source code into machine code.

Identify the name of the translator that would be used for each of the statements. Each name may be used more than once.

Translates and executes the source code one line at a time.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Interpreter

questions.text:
The programmer uses translators to translate the high-level language source code into machine code.

Identify the name of the translator that would be used for each of the statements. Each name may be used more than once.

Translates the source code and will not execute it if errors are found. It instead produces a full report of the errors in the source code.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Compiler

questions.text:
The programmer uses translators to translate the high-level language source code into machine code.

Identify the name of the translator that would be used for each of the statements. Each name may be used more than once.

Translates the source code and produces an executable file.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Compiler

questions.text:
The programmer uses translators to translate the high-level language source code into machine code.

Identify the name of the translator that would be used for each of the statements. Each name may be used more than once.

Stops execution of the machine code where an error is found and will resume execution of the machine code once the error is fixed.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Interpreter
